creator: pfg@FreeBSD.org
text:
Thank you!

This is not reproducible with the BSD-licensed patch:
% patch --version
patch 2.0-12u8 FreeBSD
/usr/ports/devel/ncurses-devel% make patch
===>  Found saved configuration for ncurses-devel-5.9.20110507_1
===> Fetching all distfiles required by ncurses-devel-5.9.20110507_1 for 
building
===>  Extracting for ncurses-devel-5.9.20110507_1
=> SHA256 Checksum OK for ncurses/ncurses-5.9.tar.gz.
=> SHA256 Checksum OK for ncurses/ncurses-5.9-20110409.patch.gz.
=> SHA256 Checksum OK for ncurses/ncurses-5.9-20110416.patch.gz.
=> SHA256 Checksum OK for ncurses/ncurses-5.9-20110423.patch.gz.
=> SHA256 Checksum OK for ncurses/ncurses-5.9-20110430.patch.gz.
=> SHA256 Checksum OK for ncurses/ncurses-5.9-20110507.patch.gz.
===>  Patching for ncurses-devel-5.9.20110507_1
===>  Applying distribution patches for ncurses-devel-5.9.20110507_1
/usr/ports/devel/ncurses-devel% file work/ncurses-5.9/test/color_name.h*
work/ncurses-5.9/test/color_name.h:      C source, ASCII text
work/ncurses-5.9/test/color_name.h.orig: empty
____

Please note that if you are using 9.x, the default patch is still
GNU patch and BSD patch is not even installed.

This could be worked around in the port Makefile, although it
will be ugly.

Pedro.
